name: Sant Miquel de Balansat — circular gravel through the interior of Ibiza
name_original: Sant Miquel de Balansat — circular gravel por el interior de Ibiza
region_intro: Touring cycling around Sant Joan De Labritja offers a tranquil experience through the northern hills of Ibiza. The region is characterized by unspoiled nature, traditional architecture, and varied terrain. Cyclists will encounter quiet asphalted roads, technical sections, and routes winding through Mediterranean forests, olive groves, and traditional fields. Rugged coastal scenery provides panoramic views of hidden coves and beaches. Best touring cycling routes around Sant Joan De Labritja The most popular touring cycling route is Sant Miquel de Balansat — circular gravel through the interior of Ibiza, a 20.1 miles (32.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the island's rural interior. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Punta de Llevant y cala Xarraca, a difficult 15.3 miles (24.6 km) path. This route features rugged coastal scenery and leads to clear coves. Local touring cyclists also love the Cala Nova Beach – Es Canar Beach loop from Cala de Sant Vicent, a 21.8 miles (35.1 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Sant Joan De Labritja is defined by rugged coastal scenery, Mediterranean forests, and traditional fields. The network offers options for various ability levels, from quiet roads to routes with demanding climbs. The routes in Sant Joan De Labritja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Joan De Labritja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes in the Sant Joan De Labritja area, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: April and August are particularly popular months for touring cycling in Sant Joan De Labritja. The region offers a tranquil atmosphere and diverse landscapes, making it enjoyable for cycling throughout much of the year, especially outside the peak summer heat.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: The terrain around Sant Joan De Labritja is varied. You'll find quiet asphalted roads, but also routes with technical sections, demanding climbs, and descents through Mediterranean forests. Expect rugged coastal scenery, traditional fields, olive groves, vineyards, and almond trees.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. While many routes offer challenges, you can find easier options that allow you to enjoy the tranquil atmosphere and beautiful scenery without overly demanding climbs. There is at least one easy route available, and 15 moderate routes that can be suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Which routes offer a more challenging touring cycling experience?
A: For those seeking a challenge, there are 19 difficult touring cycling routes in the area. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012972774/punta-de-llevant-y-cala-xarraca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Llevant y cala Xarraca&lt;/a&gt;, which features rugged coastal scenery and significant elevation changes, provide a demanding ride. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19428098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Es Canar Beach – View of Cala de Sant Vicent loop from Portinatx&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 56 km with substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Sant Joan De Labritja are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009050741/sant-miquel-de-balansat-circular-gravel-through-the-interior-of-ibiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel de Balansat — circular gravel through the interior of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the island's rural interior. Another popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38211638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Nova Beach – Es Canar Beach loop from Cala de Sant Vicent&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can discover hidden coves like Cala d'en Serra and Cala Xuclar, or visit the golden sands of Cala de Sant Vicent. Inland, routes wind through Mediterranean forests and traditional fields. For specific natural landmarks, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aigües Blanques Beach&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/717417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock formations at Punta des Moscarter&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any caves or unique geological features to see?
A: Yes, the area around Sant Joan De Labritja features several interesting caves. You might encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5847493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova des Forn&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1757634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Blanca Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7188146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llevant Cave&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes may even pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4521868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green pools and waterfall at Can Marçà Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unspoiled nature, the tranquility of the northern hills, and the diverse landscapes that range from rugged coastlines to charming inland forests and traditional villages.

Q: Can I find bike rental services in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Sant Joan De Labritja and the surrounding areas are well-equipped for cyclists. You can find various bike rental services that offer both traditional and e-bikes, catering to different preferences and skill levels for exploring the region.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes?
A: While the guide highlights a range of difficulties, the region's quiet asphalted roads and less technical sections can be suitable for families. Look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' and consider the overall distance and elevation gain to ensure it matches your family's capabilities. The tranquil atmosphere away from bustling areas makes it a pleasant environment for family rides.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route?
A: Sant Joan De Labritja, being a tranquil village, typically offers parking options within or near the village center. Many routes start from accessible points, and you can often find parking in the vicinity of the main squares or designated parking areas before embarking on your cycling adventure.

Q: What are some hidden gems or less-known spots to discover by bike?
A: Beyond the well-known routes, the region is dotted with hidden gems. Cycling allows you to discover traditional 'payesa' houses, characteristic dry stone walls, and secluded spots like the tiny Cala Xuclar. The rural surroundings of Sant Joan village itself offer numerous paths and trails inviting exploration away from the main roads.
tips:
Population of the Ibizan municipality of San Juan de Labritja (Balearic Islands, Spain), located in the northeast of the island.
The church of San Miguel stands out, raised on a hill, which receives the name of Puig de Missa. It is from the 14th century, making it one of the oldest temples on the island
Lovely gravel track through a pine forest, quite steep on the northern end.
